finding.summary: Capture is 92% silence (audio.silence_fraction=0.92), so every level/loudness statistic is heavily diluted by dead air and cannot be trusted to characterize the actual backing-vocal content. The 16.5 dB gap between sample_peak_db (-22.0) and true_peak_db (-5.5) is unusually large but, in a near-silent capture, is likely driven by brief isolated transients rather than processed program material, and the empty fx_chain (fx_chain: []) rules out an in-chain limiter that would otherwise explain it. No actionable diagnosis is safe from this snapshot.
finding.probable_cause: Insufficient program material in the capture window: with silence_fraction at 0.92, integrated_lufs (-20.0), rms_db (-55.0), crest_factor_db (12.0), loudness_range_lu (8.0), and the three 1/3-octave points (100 Hz -18.0, 1000 Hz -24.0, 4000 Hz -30.0) all reflect a mostly-silent buffer and cannot be used to judge vocal tonal balance, gain staging, or dynamics. capture.scope is 'isolated_track' and isolation_verified is true, so the issue is the content of the capture, not leakage.
finding.confidence_reason: Confidence is capped at low because audio.silence_fraction is 0.920.

evidence_refs.description: 0.92 - exceeds 0.75 threshold; statistics diluted by silence
Empty FX chain - no limiter or processor present to account for peak discrepancy

proposal.reason: audio.silence_fraction is 0.92 (>= 0.75), so no level-based move on track volume, pan, FX parameters, or FX bypass can be justified from this capture. A new capture of an active section of the backing vocal is required before any single-track diagnosis or proposal is safe.
